chain_of_thought: Native stack only shows an abort with no resolved frames between abort and the JNI entrypoint Java_com_here_sdk_core_GeoCoordinates_fromString, and all intermediate frames are '??', so there is no concrete evidence of memory corruption, OOB, or allocator/sanitizer diagnostics.

The termination reason is a plain abort rather than a crash in malloc/free or a signal like SIGSEGV; this pattern is consistent with an explicit assertion failure or argument check inside the HERE SDK library when input does not meet expected format, e.g., an invalid coordinate string.

The JNI bridge method name GeoCoordinates_fromString strongly suggests it parses a string into latitude/longitude values; malformed or impossible values from the fuzzer are very likely to hit internal validation and cause an intentional abort or exception mapping, especially in a fuzz harness that bypasses higher-level app checks.

We lack decompiled native code or Java callgraph beyond the fuzz driver, so we cannot trace arguments to an unsafe memcpy/memmove or similar; the data flow we can see is fuzz_one_input → Java_com_here_sdk_core_GeoCoordinates_fromString → abort with no indication of unsafe memory use.

Given this, the most plausible explanation is a harness-driven, unrealistic input triggering a defensive abort (logic-level DoS in the harness only), not a genuine, exploitable memory-safety vulnerability reachable under normal application flows.
reasons: Abort appears to be triggered by internal checks/assertions in GeoCoordinates_fromString rather than memory corruption (no sanitizer/allocator frames, only plain abort).
Fuzzer harness directly feeds arbitrary data into a coordinate parsing JNI without normal app-level validation, so crashes likely reflect defensive aborts on invalid inputs.

No evidence of unsafe native operations (memcpy, pointer arithmetic, UAF) or tainted lengths/pointers; stack frames between abort and JNI entry are unresolved, and library map only shows a single HERE SDK .so.

evidence.note: JNI entrypoint for parsing coordinates from a string; called directly by fuzz_one_input and terminates via abort with no intermediate resolved frames.
Fuzz harness entry passes arbitrary data into JNI, likely bypassing normal validation present in production Java code.

recommendations: Obtain and decompile libheresdk.so around GeoCoordinates_fromString to verify whether abort is due to explicit assert/validation or hidden memory-safety issues.

Adjust the fuzz harness to more closely mimic real Java usage (e.g., proper string construction and range checks) and capture any exception mapping instead of treating every abort as a security issue.
If source or symbols are available, instrument GeoCoordinates_fromString with logging or assertions that distinguish between expected invalid-input failures and unintended bugs.
assumptions: GeoCoordinates_fromString is a pure parsing/validation routine that may intentionally abort on malformed input.
The fuzzer provides highly malformed or non-UTF-8 data that realistic app flows would not pass to this JNI without pre-checks.
No hidden sanitizer output or allocator diagnostics were omitted from the provided crash entry.
limitations: No disassembly or decompiled view of GeoCoordinates_fromString, so internal implementation and checks are unknown.
Intermediate native frames between abort and JNI entry are unresolved ('??'), preventing precise localization of the abort point.
No Java callgraph or app code around the HERE SDK usage was provided to confirm normal validation and reachable inputs.
